content.text:
Virtualization and Cloud Computing
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEM2  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lecture:
1h30  |
Lab:
3h
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
The Virtualisation & Cloud Computing module aims to help students understand the basic concepts of cloud computing and virtualisation, as well as associated technologies and tools. Students will be able to deploy and manage applications on the Cloud, understand different deployment and service models, monitor performance, and ensure data security and compliance.
Course Content (21h)
Introduction to Cloud computing and virtualisation
Basic concepts of Cloud computing and virtualisation
Different types of clouds (public, private, hybrid) and service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS)
Virtualisation protocols (hypervisors, containers)
Virtualisation technologies:
Containerisation technologies (Docker, Kubernetes)
Hypervisors (VMware, Hyper-V)
Cloud management tools:
Cloud management tools (OpenStack, CloudStack)
Cloud monitoring tools (Nagios, Zabbix)
Configuration management tools (Ansible, Chef)
Deploying applications on the Cloud:
Deployment on public cloud (AWS, Azure, GCP)
Deployment on private cloud (OpenStack, VMware)
Reference architectures for Cloud applications
Managing VMs and containers
Security and compliance in the Cloud:
Security challenges in the Cloud
Best security practices for Cloud resources
Regulations and compliance standards for Cloud data
Trends and evolutions in Cloud computing and virtualisation:
Current market trends and new technologies
Future prospects for Cloud computing and virtualisation
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — tests, personal work
